authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(10) — Berryessa Lake tributaries (Lake and Napa Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (10) Berryessa Lake tributaries (Lake and Napa Cos.). Last Sat. in Apr. through Sep. 30. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS Domestic Names (California) bulk file — record for gaz_id 255067, "Lake Berryessa", Reservoir, Napa Co., CA
sources.quotedText: 255067|Lake Berryessa|Reservoir|California|06|Napa|055|Lake Berryessa|06/14/2000|04/05/2023|Official|Congressional Legislation|01/01/1956|383517N|1221343W|38.5879387|-122.2286929|||0.0|0.0 — this GNIS reservoir record (gaz_id 255067, not present in the live carto.nationalmap.gov ArcGIS MapServer/7 mirror at time of check, but present in the official USGS GNIS Domestic Names bulk distribution for CA) is used as the anchor coordinate for these Lake Berryessa tributary mouths; converts to lat 38.5879387, lon -122.2286929, matching the file's corrected coordinate. This replaced the file's prior coordinate (-122.4200, 38.7000), which was ~20km away (outside the lake's own bounding box) and unsupported by any GNIS-backed source.

regulations.humanSummary: Open last Saturday in April through September 30
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr. through Sep. 30.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout daily bag limit, 4 trout in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag limit, 4 trout in possession

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
